audience: Engineers onboarding to the deployment process

mode_descriptions.staging: Auto-deploy on merge to main, no approval gate, runs against staging DB
mode_descriptions.prod: Manual approval required, blue-green deploy, runs against production RDS

flows.name: Deploy on Merge
flows.purpose: Main deploy flow: PR merged → CI builds → image pushed → ArgoCD syncs → cluster updated.
step_outline: dev → ci       : push / merge triggers workflow
ci → ci        : lint, test, build (internal step)
ci → registry  : docker push tagged image
ci → deploy    : update manifests / trigger sync
deploy → cluster : rolling update (staging) or blue-green (prod)

flows.name: Rollback
flows.purpose: Something broke in production. Revert to previous known-good image.
step_outline: dev → deploy    : trigger rollback via CLI or UI
deploy → registry : fetch previous image tag

flows.name: Hotfix (prod-only)
flows.purpose: Emergency patch: skip staging, fast-track to prod with approval.

lessons: Staging and prod share the same pipeline shape but differ in approval gates and cluster size. This is a good use of modes — same flow, different deployment context.
The rollback flow is short because it reuses an existing image. No build step.
Hotfix is prod-only because it bypasses staging entirely — use onlyMode to enforce this.
